01 — ABOUT ME

WHO I AM

I'm a second-year Mechanical Engineering student at HAN University of Applied Sciences in Arnhem, the Netherlands. I have a strong interest in robotics, aviation, and innovative engineering systems — particularly where mechanics, design, and real-world problem-solving come together.

Through my academic projects, I've gained experience in CAD design, prototyping, mechanical analysis, and testing. I enjoy turning theoretical knowledge into practical solutions and continuously challenge myself to improve both technically and professionally.

With a growing focus on robotics and innovative systems, I'm especially driven by the connection between smart design and real-world performance. The ability to translate theoretical concepts into practical solutions makes me a motivated and reliable contributor within multidisciplinary teams.

Seeking Internship — September 2026
HAN University of Applied Sciences, Arnhem
Robotics Aviation Drones Mechanical Design AR/VR
English (Fluent) Portuguese (Native) Cape Verdean Creole Dutch (A2) Spanish (B2)
02 — SELECTED WORK

PROJECTS

01
Mechanical Battery
Designed and developed a fully mechanical energy storage system converting gravitational potential energy from a 2 kg mass into stored torsional spring energy, subsequently releasing it to lift a 1 kg mass. Covered concept design, engineering calculations, CAD, manufacturing, and proof-of-principle testing.
SolidWorks Torsional Spring Gear Ratio Optimization FEA Manufacturing
↗
02
Robotic Arm Gripper — Cobot CR10
As team leader, I led the full development of a custom gripper system for the CR10 collaborative robot. I personally designed and 3D printed all custom gripper fingers and fixtures to ensure precise, stable handling of cylindrical and ring-shaped parts. Programmed in DobotStudio using Lua scripting, controlling sorting and placement logic based on colour detection. Zero positioning errors.
Team Lead DobotStudio (Lua) Dobot CR10 3D Printing Fixture Design SolidWorks
↗
03
Windmill-Powered Water Pump
Designed and built a windmill-powered water pump system capable of pumping up to three meters. Developed mechanical drawings, performed structural calculations on the main shaft, and validated the blade design with CFD analysis. Manufacturing involved welding and turning.
SolidWorks CFD Stress Analysis Welding Turning
↗
04
Lightweight Bike & Dummy System ONGOING
Designing and developing a lightweight bike and dummy system with minimal material usage while maintaining structural stability. Focused on material selection and structural optimization.
Structural Analysis Material Optimization CAD
↗
05
AR Environment — Aryzon.world Cell
Created an augmented reality environment of a U-shaped manufacturing cell using Meta Quest 3. Every component was modelled in CAD and integrated into the AR workspace, providing an interactive digital twin of the physical setup.
Meta Quest 3 ARYZON.world Digital Twin CAD
↗
